response: {"calibration_score":0.32,"overconfident_count":3,"underconfident_count":1,"well_calibrated_count":9,"biases_detected":["anchoring: repeated assumptions about server port availability (localhost:3006/5006) without prior liveness check, leading to predictable curl failures in units -20 and -27","path-assumption bias: multiple bash commands run without explicit cd or absolute path, causing failures when CWD differed from expected project root","sunk-cost: jq aggregator debugging in unit -8 iterated through 6+ tool calls (Write fail, hex inspection, non-ASCII grep, re-Write) rather than switching to a simpler inline approach earlier","recency: unit -24 attempted a sleep-poll on a task output path derived from session context rather than verifying the path existed first"],"recommendations":["probe environment preconditions before dependent commands — one lsof/curl check at task start to establish which ports are live avoids 3-5 redundant failure-recovery cycles per session","normalize CWD at task entry with a single explicit cd or use absolute paths throughout; relative-path bash commands are the leading cause of recoverable failures in this session","for iterative debugging (jq, shell scripts), apply a 3-strike rule: if the same approach fails twice, switch strategy rather than re-diagnosing the same artifact","tag task output file paths as ephemeral and verify existence before polling; prefer TaskOutput MCP tool over direct file tail for in-flight task monitoring"]}
